title: Autonomous Revenue Cycle Agent for Independent Medical Practices
one_liner: AI agent that handles the entire billing-to-collection workflow for small medical practices
problem_statement: Independent medical practices lose 15-30% of revenue to billing errors, claim denials, and slow collections. The average practice has 2.5 FTEs dedicated to billing, costing $150K+ annually.
solution_concept: An agentic AI system that autonomously manages the entire revenue cycle: claim generation, submission, denial management, patient billing, and collections follow-up.
why_now: CMS interoperability mandates (2024-2025) have opened payer data APIs. LLM capabilities now handle the multi-step reasoning required for medical coding and payer rule navigation.
why_agentic: Revenue cycle management requires multi-step reasoning across coding guidelines (ICD-10, CPT), payer-specific rules, denial patterns, and patient communication - a perfect fit for agentic AI.
market_signal: $15B revenue cycle management market growing at 12% CAGR. 250,000+ independent practices in the US alone.
moat_hypothesis: Proprietary data flywheel from processing claims across practices creates increasingly accurate coding and denial prediction models.
biggest_risk: Healthcare regulatory compliance (HIPAA, state billing regulations) creates high barriers but also high liability.
recommended_first_customer: Solo dermatology practices billing $500K-2M annually in states with simpler billing regulations
